title: Radio Bart

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature reaction to frustration
jokes.punchline: The iconic Homer catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly economical and character-specific

jokes.text: Hey! Where in God's name did you learn that kind of... dancing?
jokes.setup: Homer's outrage building toward something inappropriate
jokes.punchline: The pause before 'dancing' reveals it's actually innocent
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection with the pause, makes audience think something worse

jokes.text: Dad, this is $110. - Oh. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Homer casually giving what seems like pocket change
jokes.punchline: It's actually a huge amount of money he didn't realize
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness to money, good character moment

jokes.text: That could be Bart.
jokes.setup: Watching TV ad for radio microphone
jokes.punchline: Homer imagining Bart as a trucker on CB radio
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical misunderstanding of what the product does

jokes.text: Hey, good-looking... we'll be back to pick you up later. He's in for some loving.
jokes.setup: Radio microphone commercial
jokes.punchline: Absurd sexual innuendo in what should be a kids' toy ad
jokes.explanation: Good absurd moment, inappropriate content in kids' commercial

jokes.text: It won't be like those shoe trees... I got him last year... or the shelf paper I bought him for Christmas. I'll buy his love yet.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on past terrible gift choices
jokes.punchline: Escalating from practical to completely inappropriate gifts, then darkly honest about buying love
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from bad to worse gifts, dark honest ending about buying love

jokes.text: Wow. Best eight bucks I ever spent.
jokes.setup: Bart's name being read on Krusty's show
jokes.punchline: Homer proud of such a small achievement for such little money
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment showing his low standards for parental achievement

jokes.text: Take a message. Right now, I'm off to hit 46 local merchants... for free birthday... goods and services.
jokes.setup: Grandpa calling to wish happy birthday
jokes.punchline: Bart ignoring family for calculated birthday scamming
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart scheming, specific number makes it funnier

jokes.text: I'm here for my free birthday sundae. Eat it and get out.
jokes.setup: Bart collecting free birthday treats
jokes.punchline: Clerk's blunt hostility
jokes.explanation: Good economy in the rude response, captures service industry attitude

jokes.text: Digital audiotape, my butt. When I was a kid, we had compact discs... and I don't recall no one complaining. Damn right.
jokes.setup: Old men complaining about new technology
jokes.punchline: Grandpa thinks CDs are old technology, backwards timeline
jokes.explanation: Smart reversal - Grandpa thinks CDs are the old reliable technology

jokes.text: Hey, later, okay? Hey, why don't you go bug your parents?
jokes.setup: Kids expecting party entertainer to be entertaining
jokes.punchline: Professional entertainer telling kids to go away
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectations for children's entertainer

jokes.text: Hell, no. - You got it.
jokes.setup: Being asked if he wants a special birthday song
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt rejection and worker's cheerful compliance
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy, captures everyone's hatred of birthday songs

jokes.text: Before we got it... I was always finding Patty's stubble... in my leg razor.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they needed a label maker
jokes.punchline: Gross overshare about Patty's body hair in Marge's razor
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment, too much information from Marge

jokes.text: Next.
jokes.setup: Receiving birthday presents
jokes.punchline: Bart dismissively moving through gifts like a bureaucrat
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy showing Bart's ungrateful attitude

jokes.text: Ooh,just gorgeous. Now we can be twins.
jokes.setup: Giving Bart a present
jokes.punchline: Giving him feminine clothing so they can match
jokes.explanation: Classic Patty/Selma inappropriate gift, very character-specific

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he gave away the last beer
jokes.punchline: Another signature D'oh moment
jokes.explanation: Another perfect D'oh usage, Homer's beer priorities

jokes.text: Hey, good-looking... we'll be back for some dinner later.
jokes.setup: Homer using the microphone
jokes.punchline: Repeating the inappropriate commercial line
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier commercial, Homer not understanding appropriateness

jokes.text: Mmm, chocolate. D'oh! Mmm, chocolate. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer approaching ice cream repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Cycle of desire and disappointment as ice cream runs out
jokes.explanation: Good repetition showing Homer's predictable cycle of gluttony and disappointment

jokes.text: People of Earth, this is Bartron-- commander of the Martian invasion force. Your planet is in our hands. Resistance is useless. We have captured your president. He was delicious.
jokes.setup: Bart doing alien invasion prank
jokes.punchline: Escalating to cannibalism with 'He was delicious'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from standard invasion to cannibalism, perfect Bart mischief

jokes.text: Sorry, lady. Show's over.
jokes.setup: Maggie appearing to say 'Mama' for first time
jokes.punchline: Bart cruelly revealing it was just him with the microphone
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly cruel Bart moment, destroying a precious family milestone. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: I dreamed I was married to Corey... and we lived on a pony farm... and Corey was always walking around... with his shirt off. Oh, brother.
jokes.setup: Lisa sharing her dream
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate romantic content from a child, Bart's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Lisa's inappropriate romantic fantasy

jokes.text: I dreamed I was married to Corey and we lived on a pony farm and Corey was always walking around with his shirt off.
jokes.setup: Lisa sharing a dream
jokes.punchline: Innocent child having romantic dream about teen heartthrob
jokes.explanation: Age-appropriate but slightly mature dream for young Lisa - captures childhood crushes on celebrities

jokes.text: Rod, Tod, this is God. How did you get on the radio? What do you mean? I created the universe, stupid kids.
jokes.setup: Bart pretending to be God to the Flanders kids
jokes.punchline: God calling children stupid for not understanding omnipotence
jokes.explanation: Excellent blasphemous humor, God being impatient and insulting. Great Bart scheme.

jokes.text: Walk through the wall. I will remove it... for you... ...later.
jokes.setup: Testing the Flanders boys' faith
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie about removing the wall 'later'
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of God prank, the pause makes the lie obvious

jokes.text: Look, do you want a happy God or a vengeful God? Happy God. Then quit flapping your lip.
jokes.setup: Kids questioning God's cookie request
jokes.punchline: God threatening vengeance over cookies, crude language
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - God being petty and threatening over cookies. Perfect Bart manipulation.

jokes.text: Help me, please. I fell down the well! I'll get help, laddie. A little nip of courage.
jokes.setup: Bart's fake cry for help from well
jokes.punchline: Willie using rescue as excuse to drink
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie character moment - finding excuse to drink during emergency

jokes.text: Out of my way! Look out, you horse's arse! Sucker.
jokes.setup: Willie rushing to help with emergency
jokes.punchline: Willie's aggressive rescue attempt, Bart's satisfaction at fooling him
jokes.explanation: Good Willie characterization with the crude language and aggressive helping

jokes.text: My name is Timmy O'Toole. I just turned ten years old. I have no family. I tried to enroll in school... but Principal Skinner turned me away... because of my shabby clothes.
jokes.setup: Bart creating fake identity
jokes.punchline: Increasingly tragic backstory designed for maximum sympathy
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating sob story, each detail more manipulative than the last

jokes.text: He's a liar.
jokes.setup: Timmy's claims about Skinner rejecting him
jokes.punchline: Skinner's flat denial without explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response, Skinner's blunt honesty cuts through the manipulation

jokes.text: By God, men, you're a bunch of marshmallows. Why don't you go, Chief? Well, I'm too... ...important.
jokes.setup: Criticizing police for not being small enough to rescue boy
jokes.punchline: Chief's excuse that he's too important, not too fat
jokes.explanation: Good Chief Wiggum moment - avoiding work with transparent excuse

jokes.text: By God, men, you're a bunch of marshmallows. Why don't you go, Chief? Well, I'm too... important.
jokes.setup: Police can't fit down well to rescue boy
jokes.punchline: Chief Wiggum's transparently selfish excuse
jokes.explanation: Classic cowardly authority figure behavior - Chief Wiggum's self-importance overriding duty

jokes.text: I don't think he's coming back.
jokes.setup: Bird flying away instead of rescuing boy
jokes.punchline: Chief's obvious observation delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Standard Wiggum stating-the-obvious moment

jokes.text: Although we can't reach the boy... we can freeze him with liquid nitrogen... so that future generations can rescue him.
jokes.setup: Scientific solution to rescue problem
jokes.punchline: Completely impractical solution that defeats the purpose
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd scientific 'solution' that makes no sense

jokes.text: With this hook and this hunk of chocolate I'll land your boy and I'll clean him for free.
jokes.setup: Another rescue attempt being proposed
jokes.punchline: Treating trapped child like a fish to be caught and cleaned
jokes.explanation: Dark and absurd - completely inappropriate application of fishing techniques to child rescue, disturbing yet funny

jokes.text: How does that make him a hero? Well, it's more than you did.
jokes.setup: Bart questioning why being trapped makes someone heroic
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical comparison putting down his son
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - being accidentally trapped is better than nothing

jokes.text: I wanted to do something... to help... so I called my friend, Sting. He said... 'When do you need me?' I said, 'Thursday.' He said, 'I'm busy.' I said, 'Friday?' He said... 'Friday's worse.' Then he said, 'How about Saturday?' I said, 'Fine.' True story.
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining how he got Sting for charity song
jokes.punchline: Mundane scheduling conversation presented as inspiring, 'True story' tag
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of celebrity charity mystique with mundane scheduling reality

jokes.text: Yeah. I used to open for Krusty in '69. In fact, he fired me, as I recall. Oh, Sting. But this isn't about show business. This is about some kid down a hole... or something.
jokes.setup: Sting sharing history with Krusty
jokes.punchline: Vague dismissal of the actual cause they're supporting
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity satire - being vague about the actual cause

jokes.text: We got to pay for promotion, shipping, distribution. You know, those limos out back aren't free. Whatever's left, we throw down the well.
jokes.setup: Asked about charity money distribution
jokes.punchline: Admitting most goes to overhead, literally throwing remainder away
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of celebrity charity corruption, absurd ending with literally throwing money down well

jokes.text: Authentic Timmy O'Toole baby teeth. $6.00 a bag.
jokes.setup: Exploiting tragedy for profit
jokes.punchline: Selling obviously fake baby teeth as memorabilia
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd exploitation of tragedy, perfect satire of disaster merchandising

jokes.text: It's very simple, Kent. The child is reverting to a feral or wolf-like state.
jokes.setup: Expert explaining boy's condition
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous pseudo-scientific diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Good absurd expert analysis, typical TV pseudoscience

jokes.text: I was trying to gnaw my foot off, but... I couldn't get through my sock. Good night, everybody.
jokes.setup: Timmy explaining his desperate situation
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of self-amputation, cheerful sign-off
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy escalation - casual self-harm followed by cheerful goodbye. Excellent craft.

jokes.text: When they find out you've been fooling them... they're gonna want to cut you up with rusty razors. And how are they going to find out? The police will catch you. The police couldn't catch a cold.
jokes.setup: Lisa warning about consequences
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive assessment of police competence
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic, Bart's cocky dismissal of authority

jokes.text: Maybe not, but you're stupid enough... to have left a 'Bart Simpson' label... on that radio. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Lisa revealing Bart left evidence
jokes.punchline: Bart realizing his mistake with D'oh
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with Lisa outsmarting Bart, good callback to label maker gift

jokes.text: Help! I fell down the well. Tell us something we don't know.
jokes.setup: Bart genuinely calling for help
jokes.punchline: Police assume it's still the prank
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - real emergency dismissed as continued prank

jokes.text: Look, I'll level with you. There is no Timmy O'Toole. It was just a prank I was playing on everybody. Well, you sure fooled us, kid. Hey, I got an idea for a prank. Let's go home... and go to sleep. Good one, Eddie.
jokes.setup: Bart confessing the truth
jokes.punchline: Police treating his genuine confession as part of the prank, proposing sleep as a prank
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of police incompetence - treating truth as lie, sleep as prank

jokes.text: You must think we're the worst parents in the world. Yes, that's pretty much the feeling down at the station.
jokes.setup: Marge's self-deprecating worry about parenting
jokes.punchline: Lou's brutally honest confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan cruelty from Lou, no sugar-coating the public opinion

jokes.text: I brought you your Krusty doll. Ow! Knock it off, you bald boob! Don't make me come down there! Like to see you fit. Why, you little...!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort Bart with a gift
jokes.punchline: Bart hitting him with it, Homer's threat despite obvious impossibility
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer-Bart dynamic, Homer's impossible threat shows his character

jokes.text: I like the other boy. So polite.
jokes.setup: Comparing real Bart to fake Timmy
jokes.punchline: Preferring the fake polite version to the real child
jokes.explanation: Good ironic preference for the fake polite child over the real one

jokes.text: Had he done it at the start of the fiscal year, no problemo.
jokes.setup: Explaining why city won't pay for rescue
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic timing as reason for abandoning child
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic absurdity - fiscal calendar more important than human life

jokes.text: It's not our fault. We didn't want the boy. He was an accident. Homer! Could you edit that last part? Mr. Simpson, we're live. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer defending their parenting on TV
jokes.punchline: Escalating admissions culminating in live TV mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating cringe sequence - each admission worse than the last, classic Homer obliviousness to being live

jokes.text: One plus one plus three is five. Little Bart Simpson's buried alive. He's so neat. He's so sweet. Now the rats have Bart to eat...
jokes.setup: Children's playground chant about Bart
jokes.punchline: Escalating to graphic imagery of rats eating him
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark children's rhyme, escalates from cute to horrifying

jokes.text: Ever since I called for the rescue... of that Simpson lad, I have taken heat... so I am flip-flopping. I say let him stay down there.
jokes.setup: Political pressure over rescue decision
jokes.punchline: Politician abandoning child to save political career
jokes.explanation: Perfect political cowardice - abandoning child when politically convenient

jokes.text: You'll grow into it.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining his sweater is too big
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical response while he's trapped underground
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge maternal response - practical knitting logic applied to emergency situation

jokes.text: That's all I can stands. I can't stands no more. I'm going to get you out of there myself.
jokes.setup: Homer's breaking point hearing Bart's despair
jokes.punchline: Popeye reference showing Homer's heroic moment
jokes.explanation: Good Popeye reference marking Homer's transition to heroic father

jokes.text: smoke a cigarette... use a fake I.D.... shave a swear word in my hair--
jokes.setup: Bart listing things he'll never get to do
jokes.punchline: Escalating from mild to creative rebellion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart bucket list - escalates from typical teen rebellion to creative vandalism of his own body

jokes.text: The Lincoln squirrel has been assassinated. We'll stay with the story all night, if we have to.
jokes.setup: News bulletin interrupting rescue coverage
jokes.punchline: Treating squirrel death as major breaking news
jokes.explanation: Good media satire - news priorities completely backwards, treating animal death as assassination

jokes.text: Gentlemen, this canary died of natural causes. Back in the hole!
jokes.setup: Canary dies in mine shaft gas test
jokes.punchline: Chief's ridiculous denial of obvious gas danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chief Wiggum incompetence - ignoring obvious safety warning

jokes.text: Actually, I don't know if I've ever heard Bart play your albums. Shh! Marge, he's a good digger.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning Sting's fan claim
jokes.punchline: Homer prioritizing digging help over honesty
jokes.explanation: Good Homer pragmatism - not caring about truth when he needs help

jokes.text: Actually, I don't know if I've ever heard Bart play your albums. Shh! Marge, he's a good digger.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out Bart isn't really a Sting fan
jokes.punchline: Homer prioritizing manual labor over honesty
jokes.explanation: Good Homer practicality - willing to lie to celebrity to keep free labor, perfectly in character

tmdb_overview: Bart fools the town into thinking there's a little boy trapped in a well.

guest_stars.name: Pamela Hayden
guest_stars.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Rod Flanders / Janey Powell (voice)

guest_stars.name: Russi Taylor
guest_stars.character: Martin Prince / Singing Kid in Commercial (voice)

insight: Radio station gimmick drives 59 jokes in 22 minutes, anchoring a solid mid-tier episode.
